preamble: The hardening of [h] to [b] is a notable feature of certain language-wide morphological patterns, where a weakened glottal sound reverts to a stronger labial ancestor.

examples.note: Rendaku: sequential voicing and fortition of historical [p] > [h] back to [b].
Post-nasal fortition of labial glides/fricatives (acoustically similar to h) to [b].

sources: Tsujimura, N. (2013). An Introduction to Japanese Linguistics.
Nurse, D., & Hinnebusch, T. J. (1993). Swahili and Sabaki.
